spec_match.rationale: Implements the chromestatus-cited TOC sidebar and content scenario with concurrent smooth scrollIntoView calls on independent scroll containers.

interactive.rationale: TOC entries, the random control, and the simulator checkbox all drive real scrollIntoView calls and update live movement/readout details.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page frames the exact docs-sidebar synchronization problem described in the ChromeStatus motivation.

edge_cases.rationale: The simulator now waits for the article scrollend before issuing the TOC scroll, reports already-visible no-ops, and keeps both scroll containers active on narrow viewports.

references.rationale: Direct ChromeStatus and CSSOM View links are present.

design_system.rationale: Demo CSS uses shared variables, keeps button color paired with background, and wraps the live status readout on mobile.

summary: Reproduced the old issue with chrome-devtools-mcp at /v130/concurrent-smooth-scrollintoviews/toc-sync/?verify=before-pre130-sequential: the checkbox did not await the first scrollend and narrow layout collapsed the internal scrollers. Verified the fix in Chrome 148.0.0.0 at ?verify=after-pre130-listener-order: concurrent mode moved both scrollers in one run, simulator mode waited for article scrollend before the TOC scrollend on desktop and mobile, already-visible cases were labelled, mobile kept article/toc scrollable, and the status block did not overflow. The feature conformance page at /v130/concurrent-smooth-scrollintoviews/conformance/?verify=after-toc-sync-sequential reported 5 PASS / 0 FAIL with no console errors.
